briefing.title: Break the satellite, then impersonate it

sections.h: ① Telemetry beacon — the satellite's "I'm fine" broadcast
sections.p: DEMOSAT continuously downlinks a telemetry beacon reporting its health — mode, battery %, sun-tracking, comm link. The ground-station dashboard simply displays whatever beacon it hears.

sections.h: ② The trust — it can't tell real from fake
sections.p: The dashboard has no way to prove the beacon truly came from DEMOSAT. There is no authentication — whatever telemetry it receives, it believes. That blind trust is the whole vulnerability.
You uplink adcs_torque with an abused value (999 mNm). The reaction wheels overspin, the satellite tumbles, the panels lose the sun, power collapses — and the ground-station alarm fires.
A rogue drone impersonates DEMOSAT and replays a forged "healthy" beacon. Every telemetry field is a lie: NOMINAL, battery 98%, sun-tracking, comm connected.

sections.h: ⑤ Why it works — the spoof beats the alarm
sections.p: The dashboard believes the forged beacon and the alarm goes quiet — while the real satellite keeps tumbling and the physical motors never stop. The operator never sees the crisis.

briefing.ack: I understand I'll first break the satellite by uplink, then hide the damage by spoofing a forged healthy beacon.

extras.label: ④ DRONE SPOOF → HIDE THE ALARM
